1→# Development Specification: Guest Public Channel View
     2→

     4→

     6→
     7→> **Unified Backend Reference:** This feature's backend classes are part of the shared Harmony backend defined in [`unified-backend-architecture.md`](./unified-backend-architecture.md).    856→### 5.5 Rationale
   857→
   858→These states were chosen to show the phases a guest can be for viewing a public channel, the states handle critical edge cases a guest can experience since the endpoints are publicily accessible such as trying to visit a private channel or channel that isn't cached. The state also has no login redirect due to the fact that this feature is supposed allow anonymous users to access public channels. Importantly each state has a clear end to each phase so the guest ins't stuck in a loop state that they can't get out off.

  2425→### 12.6 Bot and Abuse Protection
  2426→
  2427→| Protection | Implementation | Threshold |

  2433→
  2434→### 12.7 Customer-Visible Privacy Policy Points
  2435→
  2436→- Messages in public channels are visible to anyone, including search engines
  2437→- Your display name and avatar appear with your messages in public channels
  2438→- You can opt out of public display by setting your profile to private
  2439→- We do not track or store identifying information about anonymous viewers
  2440→- Search engines may cache public content; cached content remains after channel is made private
  2441→
  2442→### 12.8 Guest User Restrictions
  2443→
  2444→| Action | Allowed | Notes |
  2445→|--------|---------|-------|
  2446→| View public channel messages | Yes | Core feature |
  2447→| View public channel attachments | Yes | If attachment is in a public channel |
  2448→| Navigate between public channels | Yes | Via server sidebar |
  2449→| Copy message permalink | Yes | Client-side only |
  2450→| Share message/channel link | Yes | Client-side only |
  2451→| Send messages | No | Requires authentication |
  2452→| React to messages | No | Requires authentication |
  2453→| View private channels | No | Returns 403/404 |
  2454→| View member list | No | Privacy protection |
  2455→| Access user profiles | No | Only public display name and avatar shown inline |
  2456→| Download message history | No | Not exposed to guests |
  2457→| Use search within channel | No | Not available for guests (future feature) |
  2458→
  2459→### 12.9 Rationale 
  2460→
  2461→The security and privacy answers obvious concerns for handling messages that are publicily accessible to anyone. The restriction on guest users are enforced for security purposes such as not being able to interact with the channel without verifying who you are, meaning public channels are read only. Privacy purposes users who send messages in public channels can opt out of revealing their profile information and instead have it be anonymous.

  2465→## 13. Risks to Completion
  2466→
  2467→### 13.1 Technology Risks
  2468→
  2469→| Technology | Learning Curve | Design Difficulty | Implementation | Verification | Maintenance |
  2470→|------------|----------------|-------------------|----------------|--------------|-------------|
  2471→| T3: Next.js SSR | Medium | Medium | Medium | Medium | Medium |
  2472→| T11: CloudFlare Edge | Medium | High | Medium | High | Low |
  2473→| T17: sanitize-html | Low | Low | Low | Medium | Low |
  2474→| T18: schema-dts | Low | Medium | Low | Medium | Low |
  2475→| T20: sharp | Low | Low | Low | Low | Low |
  2476→| T21: Lighthouse CI | Medium | Low | Medium | N/A | Low |
  2477→
  2478→### 13.2 Component Risks
  2479→
  2480→| Component | Risk | Mitigation |
  2481→|-----------|------|------------|
  2482→| SSR Performance | Slow TTFB affects SEO | Edge caching; ISR; streaming |
  2483→| Content Filtering | Regex performance on large content | Timeouts; message size limits |
  2484→| Infinite Scroll | SEO crawlers can't follow | Pagination fallback links; sitemap |
  2485→| Cache Invalidation | Stale content shown | Short TTLs; explicit invalidation |
  2486→| Bot Detection | False positives block real users | Verify bot list; appeal process |
  2487→
  2488→### 13.3 SEO-Specific Risks
  2489→
  2490→| Risk | Impact | Mitigation |
  2491→|------|--------|------------|
  2492→| JavaScript-dependent content | Crawlers may not execute JS | SSR for all content |
  2493→| Slow page load | Poor Core Web Vitals | Edge caching; image optimization |
  2494→| Duplicate content | Ranking penalty | Canonical URLs; proper pagination |
  2495→| Thin content pages | Not indexed | Minimum message threshold for indexing |
  2496→| Frequent content changes | Crawl budget waste | Last-modified headers; sitemap priority |
  2497→
  2498→### 13.4 Off-the-Shelf Considerations
  2499→
  2500→| Technology | Customization | Source | Support | Cost |
  2501→|------------|---------------|--------|---------|------|
  2502→| Next.js | SSR config, caching | Open source | Vercel paid | Free |
  2503→| CloudFlare | Edge rules, workers | SaaS | Paid tiers | $20+/mo |
  2504→| sanitize-html | None needed | Open source | Community | Free |
  2505→| Lighthouse CI | Thresholds | Open source | Community | Free |
  2506→
  2507→### 13.5 Contingency Plans
  2508→
  2509→| Risk | Trigger | Contingency |
  2510→|------|---------|-------------|
  2511→| SSR overload | > 2s TTFB p95 | Increase ISR; reduce initial messages |
  2512→| Cache stampede | Origin overload on cache miss | Stale-while-revalidate; request coalescing |
  2513→| Privacy incident | PII leaked in public view | Immediate hotfix; user notification |
  2514→| SEO ranking drop | > 20% traffic decrease | Audit with Search Console; fix issues |
  2515→
  2516→### 13.6 Rationale 
  2517→
  2518→The risks to completion covers the fact that an assessment was done on the tech stack chosen for learning curve, maintainability, and long term viability. The technology chosen are well documentated and have ongoing support, reducing any future risk for developing Harmony.

  2551→## Appendix B: Glossary

  2553→| Term | Definition |

  2564→| Guest User | Anonymous visitor without an account |
